serviceDescription: Amazon Route 53 is a cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service.

cards.title: Hosted Zones
cards.helpText: Amazon Route 53 provides highly available and scalable Domain Name System (DNS), domain name registration, and health-checking web services. It is designed to give developers and businesses an extremely reliable and cost effective way to route end users to Internet applications by translating names like example.com into the numeric IP addresses, such as 192.0.2.1, that computers use to connect to each other.
cards.bodyText: The calculations below exclude free tier discounts.

components.text: Please note that, with the exception of Route 53 Resolver, all Route 53 resources are global and only need to be accounted for in one region when estimating costs across multiple AWS regions.

components.label: info alert
components.content:
Each hosted zone has a free-tier of 10,000 records. If you need more than 10,000 records on any zone, enter them in the 'Additional Records in Hosted Zones' field below.

*Example #1:

Zone1 has 10,000 records; Zone2 has 20.000 records

In this example, user would enter 2 in Hosted Zones and 10,000 in the Additional Records field to estimate the cost. This is because Zone1 is in the free tier. Only Zone2 exceeds the 10,000 free tier so the user should enter 10,000 in the Additional Records field.

**Example #2:

Zone1 has 5,000 records; Zone2 has 10,000 records; Zone3 has 15,000 records

In this example, only Zone3 has over 10,000 records and therefore you should enter 3 in Hosted Zones and 5,000 in the Additional Records field to estimate cost.

components.label: Additional Records in Hosted Zones
components.description: Enter the sum of RRsets records in excess of 10K in each zone. See examples in the info box.
components.placeholder: Enter the sum of RRsets records in excess of 10K in each zone.

components.label: IP (CIDR) blocks
components.description: No fees are charged for storage of up to 1,000 IP blocks
